meaning: 威悉河[德意志联邦共和国北部]
examples: Soon the parade left the town and went to the Weser River.
A city of northwest Germany at the mouth of the Weser River near the north Sea.
In 1827, faced with a Weser that was silting up, Hanseatic Bremen bought the land for Bremerhaven from Hanover's king to maintain a link to the sea.
Caio Koch-Weser attracted controversy, as many felt he was not a strong enough candidate for the job, one traditionally held by a European.
Larry Summers, America's Treasury secretary, was both brave and right to insist this week (via Bill Clinton) that he will not accept Mr Koch-Weser in that post.
